memory_snapshot.content:
# Agent Instructions

You are a personal AI assistant with access to tools and persistent memory.

## Guidelines

- Always search memory before answering questions about prior conversations
- Write important facts and decisions to memory for future reference
- Use the daily log for session-level notes
- Be concise but thorough

# Heartbeat Checklist

<!-- Keep this file empty to skip heartbeat API calls.
     Add tasks below when you want the agent to check something periodically.

     Example:
     - [ ] Check for unread emails needing a reply
     - [ ] Review today's calendar for upcoming meetings
     - [ ] Check CI build status for main branch
-->

# Identity

Name: Alfred
Nature: A secure personal AI assistant

Edit this file to give your agent a custom name and personality.

# Memory

Long-term notes, decisions, and facts worth remembering.
The agent appends here during conversations.

# Workspace

This is your agent's persistent memory. Files here are indexed for search
and used to build the agent's context.

## Structure

- `MEMORY.md` - Long-term notes and facts worth remembering
- `IDENTITY.md` - Agent name, nature, personality
- `SOUL.md` - Core values and principles
- `AGENTS.md` - Behavior instructions for the agent
- `USER.md` - Information about you (the user)
- `HEARTBEAT.md` - Periodic background task checklist
- `daily/` - Automatic daily session logs
- `context/` - Additional context documents

Edit these files to shape how your agent thinks and acts.

# Core Values

- Protect user privacy and data security above all else
- Be honest about limitations and uncertainty
- Prefer action over lengthy deliberation
- Ask for clarification rather than guessing on important decisions
- Learn from mistakes and remember lessons

<!-- TOOLS.md — Environment-specific tool notes.
     This file does not control which tools are available; it is guidance only.
     The agent can update this file as it learns your setup.

     Examples:
     - SSH hosts: dev-box (Ubuntu 22.04, username: alice)
     - Camera: Canon R6 mounted at /Volumes/EOS_R
     - Default shell on remote: bash, no zsh

     Add your environment notes below (outside the comment block).
-->

# User Context

The agent will fill this in as it learns about you.
You can also edit this directly to provide context upfront.
[05:26:25] Morning tech briefing delivered on [date]. Topics covered: [list of main topics]

response.content:
Please create a plan to complete this job:

Fetch the latest tech news headlines and deliver a morning briefing. Cover major tech companies, startups, AI developments, cryptocurrency, and general technology news. Format the briefing in a clean, readable way with headlines and brief summaries. Deliver the briefing to both the chat and Telegram channels.

arguments.content:
## Morning Tech News Briefing - March 4, 2026

### 📱 Mobile & Apps
**OpenAI Developing GitHub Rival**
Prompted by recent GitHub outages, OpenAI is in the early stages of developing its own code repository. The company is considering making it available to OpenAI customers, putting ChatGPT in direct competition with Microsoft.

**Tecno's Impressive Modular Phone**
Tecno unveiled a fully functional modular phone concept at MWC 2026. The prototype features mix-and-match magnetic accessories that all appear to work on the device.

**Fortnite Returning to Google Play Globally**
Epic Games and Google have withdrawn their proposed settlement and expect to submit a revised proposal by March 4, 2026.

### 🔒 Security & Privacy
**FBI and Europol Shut Down LeakBase**
Law enforcement agencies dismantled one of the world's largest hacker forums. LeakBase had over 142,000 members and a database containing "hundreds of millions" of stolen account credentials.

### 🤖 AI Developments
**AI Wikipedia Translations Added Hallucinated Sources**
A non-profit called Open Knowledge Association has been using AI to translate Wikipedia articles, leading to "hallucinations" that included incorrect, fabricated, replaced, and unrelated sources. Wikipedia editors are now placing restrictions on OKA translators.

### 💻 Hardware & Gadgets
**Keychron's New Folding Keyboard**
Keychron announced the B11 Pro, a $64.99 wireless keyboard that folds in half. Features include a split ergonomic Alice layout, remappable keys via web app, and Bluetooth/USB-C/2.4GHz connectivity.

**Infinix Note 60 Ultra Launches**
The phone, codesigned with Pininfarina, features a unique race car-shaped wireless charger and dot matrix rear display.

### 🏠 Smart Home
**Aqara U400 Supports Samsung Home Key**
The U400 is the first smart lock to work with Samsung's new Digital Home Key, allowing tap-to-unlock using Samsung Wallet.

### 🎧 Entertainment
**Audible's New Subscription Option**
Audible announced a new "standard" tier at $8.99/month (vs $14.95 for premium). Users get one audiobook per month but lose access if they cancel.

### 📊 Legal & Antitrust
**Google Android App Store Updates**
Ongoing coverage of the Google-Epic antitrust case with new developments in app store fee structures and programs.

---

*Sources: The Verge, TechCrunch*
*Delivery attempted via chat and Telegram - both channels require configuration*

![TechCrunch's Build Mode logo](https://techcrunch.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/Build-Mode-logo.png)

### Build Mode

Build Mode is a survival guide for early-stage founders navigating the messy, high-stakes chaos of building a company from scratch. No sugarcoating. No hype. Just candid conversations and tactical advice from the people who’ve done it before and have the scars (and term sheets) to prove it. Hosted by Startup Battlefield Editor Isabelle Johannessen, you can find Build Mode here or in your favorite podcast feed every Thursday by subscribing today.

9 Episodes

Updated: Feb 26, 2026

![](https://techcrunch.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/10/Equity-2024-2.jpg)

### Equity

Equity is TechCrunch’s flagship podcast about the business of startups, unpacked by the writers who know best. Produced by Theresa Loconsolo. Edited by Kell.

1068 Episodes

Updated: Mar 4, 2026

![](https://techcrunch.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/10/strictlyvc-download-podcast-art.png)

### StrictlyVC Download

Each week, StrictlyVC’s host and TechCrunch Editor-in-Chief Connie Loizos, with Alex Gove, former journalist, VC and operating exec, review the top stories in StrictlyVC and interview a mover and shaker in the world of tech.

57 Episodes

Updated: Mar 4, 2026

arguments.content:
## Morning Tech News Briefing - March 4, 2026

### 📱 Mobile & Apps
**OpenAI Developing GitHub Rival**
Prompted by recent GitHub outages, OpenAI is in the early stages of developing its own code repository. The company is considering making it available to OpenAI customers, putting ChatGPT in direct competition with Microsoft.

**Tecno's Impressive Modular Phone**
Tecno unveiled a fully functional modular phone concept at MWC 2026. The prototype features mix-and-match magnetic accessories that all appear to work on the device.

**Fortnite Returning to Google Play Globally**
Epic Games and Google have withdrawn their proposed settlement and expect to submit a revised proposal by March 4, 2026.

### 🔒 Security & Privacy
**FBI and Europol Shut Down LeakBase**
Law enforcement agencies dismantled one of the world's largest hacker forums. LeakBase had over 142,000 members and a database containing "hundreds of millions" of stolen account credentials.

### 🤖 AI Developments
**AI Wikipedia Translations Added Hallucinated Sources**
A non-profit called Open Knowledge Association has been using AI to translate Wikipedia articles, leading to "hallucinations" that included incorrect, fabricated, replaced, and unrelated sources. Wikipedia editors are now placing restrictions on OKA translators.

### 💻 Hardware & Gadgets
**Keychron's New Folding Keyboard**
Keychron announced the B11 Pro, a $64.99 wireless keyboard that folds in half. Features include a split ergonomic Alice layout, remappable keys via web app, and Bluetooth/USB-C/2.4GHz connectivity.

**Infinix Note 60 Ultra Launches**
The phone, codesigned with Pininfarina, features a unique race car-shaped wireless charger and dot matrix rear display.

### 🏠 Smart Home
**Aqara U400 Supports Samsung Home Key**
The U400 is the first smart lock to work with Samsung's new Digital Home Key, allowing tap-to-unlock using Samsung Wallet.

### 🎧 Entertainment
**Audible's New Subscription Option**
Audible announced a new "standard" tier at $8.99/month (vs $14.95 for premium). Users get one audiobook per month but lose access if they cancel.

### 📊 Legal & Antitrust
**Google Android App Store Updates**
Ongoing coverage of the Google-Epic antitrust case with new developments in app store fee structures and programs.

---

*Sources: The Verge, TechCrunch*
*Delivery attempted via chat and Telegram - both channels require configuration*

response.content:
## Job Status Report

**Job: morning-tech-news** - ⚠️ Partially Complete

### ✅ Completed:
- Successfully fetched tech news from The Verge and TechCrunch
- Compiled comprehensive morning briefing covering:
  - OpenAI developing GitHub rival
  - FBI/Europol shutting down LeakBase hacker forum
  - AI Wikipedia translation hallucinations
  - Keychron's folding keyboard
  - Tecno's modular phone concept
  - Fortnite returning to Google Play
  - Aqara smart lock with Samsung Home Key
  - Audible's new subscription tier
  - Google-Android antitrust updates
- Saved briefing to daily log

### ❌ Delivery Issues:
Both delivery channels encountered errors:

1. **Chat/Message Tool**: Requires authentication - not configured for this session
2. **Telegram Channel**: Configuration error - component implementation not found in linker

### Recommendations:
To complete delivery, the system needs:
- Authentication configuration for the `message` tool
- Proper installation/configuration of the `telegram-channel` extension

The briefing content is ready and saved. Would you like me to attempt alternative delivery methods or should the job be considered complete with the briefing saved to memory?

### 📱 Mobile & Apps
**OpenAI Developing GitHub Rival**
Prompted by recent GitHub outages, OpenAI is in the early stages of developing its own code repository. The company is considering making it available to OpenAI customers, putting ChatGPT in direct competition with Microsoft.

**Tecno's Impressive Modular Phone**
Tecno unveiled a fully functional modular phone concept at MWC 2026. The prototype features mix-and-match magnetic accessories that all appear to work on the device.

**Fortnite Returning to Google Play**
Epic Games and Google have withdrawn their proposed settlement and expect to submit a revised proposal by March 4, 2026.

### 🔒 Security & Privacy
**FBI and Europol Shut Down LeakBase**
Law enforcement agencies dismantled one of the world's largest hacker forums. LeakBase had over 142,000 members and a database containing "hundreds of millions" of stolen account credentials.

### 🤖 AI Developments
**AI Wikipedia Translations Added Hallucinated Sources**
A non-profit called Open Knowledge Association used AI to translate Wikipedia articles, leading to "hallucinations" with incorrect, fabricated, replaced, and unrelated sources. Wikipedia editors are now placing restrictions on OKA translators.

### 💻 Hardware & Gadgets

**Infinix Note 60 Ultra Launches**

arguments.content:
## ☀️ Morning Tech News Briefing - March 4, 2026

### 📱 Mobile & Apps

**OpenAI Developing GitHub Rival**
Prompted by recent GitHub outages, OpenAI is in the early stages of developing its own code repository. The company is considering making it available to OpenAI customers, putting ChatGPT in direct competition with Microsoft, a significant stakeholder in OpenAI.

**Tecno's Impressive Modular Phone**
Tecno unveiled a fully functional modular phone concept at MWC 2026. The prototype features mix-and-match magnetic accessories that all appear to work on the device.

**Fortnite Returning to Google Play**
Epic Games and Google have withdrawn their proposed settlement and expect to submit a revised proposal. Stay tuned for updates.

**Keychron's New Folding Keyboard**
Keychrom announced the B11 Pro ($64.99), a wireless keyboard that folds in half with a split ergonomic Alice layout, remappable keys, and multiple connectivity options.

---

### 🔒 Security & Privacy

**FBI and Europol Shut Down LeakBase**
Law enforcement dismantled one of the world's largest hacker forums with 142,000+ members and a database containing "hundreds of millions" of stolen account credentials.

---

### 🤖 AI Developments

**AI Wikipedia Translations Added Hallucinated Sources**
A non-profit used AI to translate Wikipedia articles, leading to fabricated and incorrect sources. Wikipedia editors are now restricting these contributions.

---

### 🏠 Smart Home

**Aqara U400 Now Supports Samsung Home Key**
The first smart lock to work with Samsung's new Digital Home Key, allowing tap-to-unlock via Samsung Wallet.

---

### 🎧 Entertainment

**Audible's New Subscription Option**
New "standard" tier at $8.99/month (vs $14.95 premium). Users get one audiobook monthly but lose access if they cancel.

---

### 📊 Legal & Antitrust

**Google Android App Store Updates**
Ongoing coverage of the Google-Epic antitrust case with developments in app store fee structures and new programs for developers.

---
*Sources: The Verge, TechCrunch*

**Job Status Update**

I've completed the research and compilation of your morning tech news briefing. Here's the briefing:

---

## 🌅 Morning Tech News Briefing - March 4, 2026

### 📱 **Mobile & Apps**

**OpenAI Developing GitHub Rival**
Prompted by recent GitHub outages, OpenAI is in the early stages of developing its own code repository. The company is considering making it available to OpenAI customers, putting ChatGPT in direct competition with Microsoft, a significant stakeholder in OpenAI.

**Tecno's Impressive Modular Phone**
Tecno unveiled a fully functional modular phone concept at MWC 2026. The prototype features mix-and-match magnetic accessories that all appear to work on the device.

**Fortnite Returning to Google Play**
Epic Games and Google have withdrawn their proposed settlement and expect to submit a revised proposal by March 4, 2026. Stay tuned for updates.

### 🔒 **Security & Privacy**

**FBI and Europol Shut Down LeakBase**
Law enforcement agencies have dismantled one of the world's largest hacker forums. LeakBase had over 142,000 members and a database containing "hundreds of millions" of stolen account credentials. The DOJ and Europol seized the forum's data and domains.

### 🤖 **AI Developments**

**AI Wikipedia Translations Added Hallucinated Sources**
A non-profit called Open Knowledge Association has been using AI to translate Wikipedia articles, leading to "hallucinations" that included incorrect, fabricated, replaced, and unrelated sources. Wikipedia editors are now placing restrictions on OKA translators.

### 💻 **Hardware & Gadgets**

**Keychron's New Folding Keyboard**
Keychron announced the B11 Pro, a $64.99 wireless keyboard that folds in half. Features include a split ergonomic Alice layout, remappable keys via web app, and Bluetooth/USB-C/2.4GHz connectivity.

**Infinix Note 60 Ultra Launches**
The phone, codesigned with Italian auto designer Pininfarina, features a unique race car-shaped wireless charger and dot matrix rear display.

### 🏠 **Smart Home**

**Aqara U400 Supports Samsung Home Key**
The U400 is the first smart lock to work with Samsung's new Digital Home Key, allowing tap-to-unlock using Samsung Wallet.

### 🎧 **Entertainment**

**Audible's New Subscription Option**
Audible announced a new "standard" tier at $8.99/month (vs $14.95 for premium). Users get one audiobook per month but don't keep access if they cancel their subscription.

### 📊 **Legal & Antitrust**

**Google Android App Store Updates**
Ongoing coverage of the Google-Epic antitrust case with new developments in app store fee structures and the Games Level Up Program.

---

**Delivery Issues:**
- ❌ Chat delivery failed - authentication required
- ❌ Telegram delivery failed - technical channel implementation error

The briefing has been saved to your daily log for reference.
